claim: JASON-OS has 13 skills: add-debt, brainstorm, checkpoint, convergence-loop, deep-plan, deep-research, pr-review, pre-commit-fixer, session-begin, session-end, skill-audit, skill-creator, todo.
evidence: D1a inventoried 7 skills (A-P), D1b inventoried 6 skills (P-T), total 13.

claim: All 13 skills are universal scope except session-begin and session-end, which are project-scoped due to SESSION_CONTEXT.md 5-field contract and JASON-OS-specific plan targets.
evidence: D1b explicitly classified session-begin and session-end as project scope. All others are universal.

claim: 2 of 13 skills are fully portable (brainstorm, convergence-loop); 9 are sanitize-then-portable; 2 are project-scoped-sanitize-then-portable; 0 are not-portable.
evidence: D1a found brainstorm and convergence-loop have no external services, no project-specific file paths, no env vars. D1b confirmed the remaining 6 skills as sanitize-then-portable.

claim: The skill byte-size spread is 20x (3,568 bytes for add-debt to 72,181 bytes for deep-research). Flat file-count agent splits will give unbalanced scanning agents in SoNash.
evidence: D1a measured byte sizes across 7 skills and identified the range.

claim: add-debt is an explicit v0 stub with a documented upgrade trigger. SoNash has a full TDMS system that this stub is designed to eventually absorb.
evidence: D1a read add-debt SKILL.md frontmatter: version '0.1-stub'. Notes explicitly document upgrade trigger.

claim: pr-review has a reference/ subdirectory (PARALLEL_AGENT_STRATEGY.md + PRE_CHECKS.md) instead of a single flat REFERENCE.md. Any glob for skill companion docs must use ** at 2+ depth.
evidence: D1a directly read the pr-review directory structure.

claim: skill-creator references several absent files: init_skill.py (Phase 4.2 scaffold), docs/agent_docs/SKILL_AGENT_POLICY.md, and the /create-audit skill. None are present in JASON-OS v0.
evidence: D1b read skill-creator SKILL.md and found references with no corresponding files. D11 confirmed as HIGH severity gaps.

claim: skill-audit SKILL.md is 599 lines, violating the 300-line limit it enforces on other skills. The version inconsistency (frontmatter v3.1, version history v4.0) is a quality flag.
evidence: D1b directly measured the SKILL.md file.

claim: JASON-OS has 8 agents (all deep-research pipeline phases) + 1 team (research-plan-team) + 0 commands. All 9 units are universal scope and fully portable.
evidence: D2 read all agent and team files. Commands directory is empty.

claim: 6 of 8 agents declare disallowedTools: Agent; 2 do not (deep-research-searcher, deep-research-synthesizer). The 2 without disallowedTools could theoretically spawn sub-agents.
evidence: D2 read YAML frontmatter of all 8 agent files.

claim: The team file (research-plan-team.md) uses HTML comment metadata, not YAML frontmatter. Any parser treating teams as agents will fail on metadata extraction.
evidence: D2 read the team file and noted the HTML comment block format.

claim: deep-research-searcher references mcp__context7 tools in its YAML tools: field — a dependency on an external MCP server distinct from agent-to-agent dependencies.
evidence: D2 read the YAML frontmatter of deep-research-searcher.md.

claim: JASON-OS has 8 hooks + 5 hook-lib helpers + settings wiring. Two hooks are hard-blocking (continueOnError: false): block-push-to-main.js and settings-guardian.js.
evidence: D3 read all hook files and the settings.json hooks section.

claim: settings-guardian.js CRITICAL_HOOKS list is hardcoded as ['block-push-to-main.js', 'settings-guardian.js']. This list must be reviewed and extended when porting to SoNash.
evidence: D3 read settings-guardian.js source code directly.

claim: run-node.sh is the universal launcher for all JASON-OS hooks. No hook fires without it. Every hook command in settings.json uses 'bash .claude/hooks/run-node.sh <script>' as the command prefix.
evidence: D3 read settings.json hooks section and all hook files.

claim: compact-restore.js uses fs.readFileSync directly (not via safe-fs) on its handoff.json read path — a compliance gap vs CLAUDE.md §2 policy.
evidence: D3 read compact-restore.js source code.

claim: JASON-OS has 12 canonical memories (1 index + 11 content files) in .claude/canonical-memory/. The memory types are: 7 feedback, 3 user, 1 project, 0 reference/tenet.
evidence: D4a read all 12 canonical memory files.

claim: user_expertise_profile.md (type: user, scope: user) contains JASON-OS-specific content: 'Stack for this project (JASON-OS) is intentionally TBD.' This content-bleed is invisible from the scope field alone.
evidence: D4a read user_expertise_profile.md body text.

claim: feedback_verify_not_grep (canonical) and feedback_grep_vs_understanding (user-home) are the same concept under different filenames. The relationship is invisible to automated sync without a supersedes field.
evidence: D4a identified the naming divergence. D4b-1 confirmed the overlapping content.

claim: The canonical MEMORY.md index does not index session-end-learnings.md. The canonical index is behind its own contents.
evidence: D4a read MEMORY.md and compared it against the directory listing.

claim: JASON-OS has 62 user-home memories. Only 10-12 have canonical counterparts, giving an ~84% gap rate. Approximately 50 portable behavioral rules are git-untracked.
evidence: D4b-1: 5/21 canonical, D4b-2: 2/21 canonical, D4b-3: 3/20 canonical. Total: ~10/62.

claim: The canonical-memory gap is thematically concentrated: skill-operational memories (deep_plan_research_check, deep_research_phases_mandatory, etc.) and agent-behavior memories (ack_requires_approval, agent_hot_reload, etc.) are entirely absent from canonical.
evidence: D4b-1 identified 3 thematic clusters of missing memories with 5 files each.

claim: t3_convergence_loops.md uses a t3_ tenet-numbering prefix convention. The file has type: reference in frontmatter but is semantically a design-philosophy tenet, not a lookup table.
evidence: D4b-3 read t3_convergence_loops.md body and analyzed its structure vs reference_pr_review_integrations.md.

claim: originSessionId is present in ~40% of user-home memories. Those that have it use either UUIDs or human-readable session labels (Session #NNN) — two incompatible formats requiring normalization.
evidence: D4b-2 documented both formats in its batch. D4b-3 confirmed the pattern.

claim: project_sync_mechanism_principles.md has an explicit Memory links: section naming 5 other memory files by filename. This is the richest memory cross-reference dependency graph in the corpus.
evidence: D4b-3 read the Memory links section of the file directly.

claim: user_creation_mindset.md is explicitly referenced by name in project_sync_mechanism_principles.md as load-bearing for the current project design. Its absence from canonical-memory is the highest-priority gap in D4b-3.
evidence: D4b-3 read both files and found the named reference.

claim: Three distinct frontmatter patterns exist across the memory corpus: minimal (name, description, type only), extended (+ status: active), and fully-attributed (+ originSessionId). Schema drift across 3 generations.
evidence: D4b-3 classified all 20 files in its batch by frontmatter pattern.

claim: JASON-OS has 13 scripts across scripts/, scripts/lib/, scripts/planning/, and scripts/planning/lib/. 7 are library helpers; 6 are entry-point scripts. All but session-end-commit.js are portable.
evidence: D5 read all 13 script files directly.

claim: scripts/log-override.js is referenced by session-end-commit.js but does not exist. The call is inside try/catch with 'non-blocking' comment — silent failure at runtime.
evidence: D5 found the reference in session-end-commit.js. D11 classified as HIGH severity gap.

claim: read-jsonl.js exists at two paths with different implementations: scripts/lib/read-jsonl.js (CJS, minimal) and scripts/planning/lib/read-jsonl.js (ESM, streaming, safer). The lib variant appears to be a legacy predecessor not yet retired.
evidence: D5 read both files and compared their implementations.

claim: safe-fs.js self-documents that it is copied verbatim into .claude/skills/*/scripts/lib/safe-fs.js as a per-skill helper. Census tooling must account for these copies to avoid treating them as distinct entries.
evidence: D5 read the safe-fs.js docstring directly.

claim: The scripts/planning/ subtree is ESM (via its own package.json with type:module). Everything else in scripts/ is CJS. The ESM/CJS boundary is crossed via createRequire in todos-cli.js and planning/lib/read-jsonl.js.
evidence: D5 read the package.json and createRequire patterns in scripts.

claim: JASON-OS statusline tool consists of 13 files (11 readable). cache.go is the canonical example for the source_scope vs runtime_scope distinction: its source is universal, its runtime behavior writes to ~/.claude/statusline/cache-jason-os/.
evidence: D6 read cache.go and identified the machine-scoped write path.

claim: The statusline cache directory uses the repo name as a namespace suffix (cache-jason-os). This establishes the per-repo isolation pattern for machine-local artifacts in a shared user home.
evidence: D6 read the cache directory path from cache.go.

claim: config.toml contains Nashville/CST timezone defaults — user-specific values that must be sanitized for generic JASON-OS distribution.
evidence: D6 read config.toml directly.

claim: JASON-OS has 4 research sessions. R-frpg (file-registry-portability-graph) produced the scope enum (5 values) and Option D recommendation that are binding inputs to Piece 2 schema design.
evidence: D7 read BRAINSTORM.md Section 3.2 which explicitly cites R-frpg.

claim: Both complete L1 research sessions use the sonash-pre-canonical-v0 schema. Any downstream consumer needs to field-map before using the structured JSONL data.
evidence: D7 read metadata.json for both sessions and found the same schemaVersion value.

claim: jason-os-mvp is a hybrid research+planning session: it produced both RESEARCH_OUTPUT.md and PHASE_3_95_PLAN.md, making it a session_type: hybrid by D7's classification.
evidence: D7 directly listed and read session file structure.

claim: PORT_ANALYSIS.md (53 KB, 33+ rows) is the most structurally significant planning artifact for schema design. It directly models a file registry with per-file dependency and portability metadata.
evidence: D8 read PORT_ANALYSIS.md header and structure.

claim: RESUME.md is a new artifact type — a session bookmark carrying git SHA, what-done summary, what-is-next pointer, and outstanding user actions. It deserves a formal plan_scope: session-bookmark classification.
evidence: D8 read RESUME.md directly.

claim: todos.jsonl has 22 records (17 pending, 5 completed). 4 todos are tagged #sync-mechanism (T2/T4/T9/T13). The context.files array per record directly useful for dependency tracing.
evidence: D8 read todos.jsonl and counted records.

claim: 6 of 7 CI workflows are fully portable. Only sonarcloud.yml requires project-specific secrets (SONAR_TOKEN) and a companion properties file (sonar-project.properties).
evidence: D9 read all 7 workflow files.

claim: settings.json permissions.deny blocks 4 destructive operations: force-push, push-to-main, reset-hard, rm-rf. CLAUDE_CODE_EXPERIMENTAL_AGENT_TEAMS=1 is set in env.
evidence: D9 read settings.json permissions and env sections.

claim: .claude/statusline-command.sh is referenced in settings.json statusLine.command but was not found in any Wave 1 scan scope. This is an unresolved gap.
evidence: D9 read settings.json. D11 listed it as a MEDIUM severity gap.

claim: Qodo is confirmed as a GitHub App (not a YAML workflow). Its presence is not visible in .github/workflows/ and must be verified at the GitHub App installation level.
evidence: D9 confirmed per CLAUDE.md §2.

claim: D11 mapped 204 total dependency edges: 178 HIGH confidence, 24 MEDIUM, 2 LOW. The graph covers all 9 Wave 1 inventory categories.
evidence: D11 produced the dependency map from all 13 Wave 1 JSONL files.

claim: scripts/lib/safe-fs.js has 14 inbound edges — the most-depended-on unit in JASON-OS. It is the universal safe I/O layer mandatory per CLAUDE.md §2 at all file-I/O boundaries.
evidence: D11 computed inbound edge count from the full dependency map.

claim: scripts/lib/sanitize-error.cjs has 10 inbound edges. It is hard-required (not optional) by the two hard-blocking hooks (large-file-gate, settings-guardian) and all planning scripts.
evidence: D11 computed inbound edges. D3 confirmed the hard-require pattern.

claim: skills/convergence-loop has 9 inbound edges — the hub of the skill infrastructure layer. It is a behavioral primitive embedded programmatically by brainstorm, deep-plan, deep-research, skill-audit, and skill-creator.
evidence: D11 computed inbound edges from D1a dependency arrays.

claim: hooks/lib/symlink-guard.js is the one cross-boundary import in the security cluster: it is in .claude/hooks/lib/ but imported by scripts/lib/safe-fs.js and scripts/lib/security-helpers.js. Cannot be moved without updating both consumers.
evidence: D11 traced the cross-boundary import. D5 confirmed it in the dependency graph.

claim: The todo skill has the deepest dependency chain in JASON-OS: 4 levels deep through scripts/planning/ — all paths converge on safe-fs.js and sanitize-error.cjs.
evidence: D11 traced the full dependency chain in Cluster D.

claim: The .claude/state/ directory functions as a shared message bus between hooks and session skills. handoff.json is the compaction bridge (pre-compaction-save writes, compact-restore reads).
evidence: D11 traced the state-file edges. D12 documented the compaction state preservation process.

claim: Three HIGH-severity gaps in the dependency map: scripts/log-override.js, init_skill.py, and .claude/statusline-command.sh are referenced but not found in inventory.
evidence: D11 Section 5 explicitly lists these as HIGH severity gaps.

claim: D12 identified 15 composite entities: 8 user-invoked workflows and 6 automatic processes and 1 cross-cutting memory system.
evidence: D12 produced the composite entity document.

claim: /deep-research is the largest composite workflow with 25 component units spanning 12 phases. It is the only workflow with optional cross-model verification (gemini-cli) and MCP dependencies (context7).
evidence: D12 enumerated all /deep-research components in its component table.

claim: The pre-push gate has a dual-layer enforcement design: .husky/pre-push (git layer) and block-push-to-main.js (Claude Code PreToolUse layer). Both independently block the operation, closing bypass gaps.
evidence: D12 explicitly documented the dual-layer design.

claim: SESSION_CONTEXT.md, RESEARCH_OUTPUT.md, and handoff.json each have implicit schema contracts (data_contracts) that are the true coupling points between composites. These contracts are currently undocumented in any schema field.
evidence: D12 Section 4 schema-field candidates surfaced data_contracts[] as an overlooked load-bearing field.

claim: Memory canonical promotion is entirely manual — no automated sync hook exists. The canonical-memory system is one of D12's 15 composites but has no programmatic trigger for promotion.
evidence: D12 Section 3 (composite 15) explicitly notes: 'No automated sync hook currently exists.'

claim: D13 consolidated 73 unique schema-field candidates from 13 Wave 1 agents. 65 were surfaced; 2 were absorbed via naming deduplication (related_memories→related, port_lineage+version_lineage→lineage).
evidence: D13 Section 7 documents the consolidation methodology.

claim: The 5-value scope enum (universal / user / project / machine / ephemeral) is confirmed load-bearing by all 13 Wave 1 agents with no edge cases requiring a 6th value.
evidence: D13 Section 1.1 aggregates all 13 agent confirmations.

claim: The 3-value portability enum (portable / sanitize-then-portable / not-portable) covered every unit type cleanly across all 13 Wave 1 agents with no edge cases.
evidence: D13 Section 1.2 aggregates confirmation from all 13 agents.

claim: D13 proposes a 12-field MVP schema: name, path, type, scope, portability, status, purpose, dependencies, lineage, supersedes+superseded_by, sanitize_fields, notes. Sufficient to answer 'should this unit be synced, and how?' for all unit types.
evidence: D13 Section 6. Confidence MEDIUM because Piece 2 has final say.

claim: The lineage field (object: source_project, source_path, source_version, ported_date) is the most important schema addition not in Wave 1 JSONL. Without it, bidirectional sync cannot track provenance.
evidence: D13 Section 3.4 documents the lineage field rationale.

claim: The supersedes + superseded_by field pair is the highest-priority gap per D4a. Without these, the feedback_verify_not_grep vs feedback_grep_vs_understanding naming divergence is an invisible sync bug.
evidence: D4a named this 'the highest-priority schema gap'. D13 confirmed at Section 3.1-3.2.

claim: A single scope field is insufficient for tools and hooks. source_scope and runtime_scope are needed: cache.go source is universal, runtime is machine. agent team definitions are universal at definition time but ephemeral at runtime.
evidence: D6 identified cache.go. D2 identified the team lifecycle issue. D13 Section 4.3-4.4 formalizes both.

claim: data_contracts[] should be a first-class schema field on composite-type entries. SESSION_CONTEXT.md, RESEARCH_OUTPUT.md, handoff.json have implicit schema contracts that are the true coupling points between composites.
evidence: D12 surfaced data_contracts in its schema-field candidates. D13 included it in the next-tier recommendations.

claim: The sanitize_fields field (array of specific config keys to replace) operationalizes portability=sanitize-then-portable. Without it, sanitization is purely manual and audit-prone.
evidence: D9 identified sonar.projectKey, sonar.organization, name as specific fields. D13 Section 5.8 formalizes.

claim: Composite entities should be first-class schema entries, not derived views. You cannot sync /deep-research by copying one file — you need to know all 25 component units.
evidence: D12 Section 5 provides 4 reasons why composites need first-class representation.

claim: feedback_no_broken_widgets.md embeds SoNash dashboard-specific content (Tabs 5 and 6, velocity-log, commit-log) inside what appears to be a universal feedback rule. Content-bleed is invisible from scope classification alone.
evidence: D4b-2 read the file body and identified the SoNash-specific references.

claim: SoNash skills are estimated at 81 vs JASON-OS 13, agents at ~40 vs 8, hooks at ~25 vs 8, memories at ~83 vs 62, scripts at ~98 vs 13. All category volumes require multi-agent splits for Piece 1b.
evidence: Per-agent SoNash estimates from each agent's Adjustments Recommended section.

claim: The t3_ tenet-numbering convention implies sibling tenets t1_, t2_, and potentially t4_+ may exist. Only t3_convergence_loops.md was found in the user-home corpus.
evidence: D4b-3 Section: 'Implied siblings: t1_, t2_, and potentially t4_+ tenets may exist elsewhere or may not yet have been written.'

claim: The cache directory naming convention (~/.claude/statusline/cache-jason-os/) uses the repo name as namespace suffix. The sync mechanism must adopt this pattern to prevent cross-contamination when JASON-OS and SoNash run on the same machine.
evidence: D6 read cache.go and explicitly noted the isolation design intent.

claim: Recency signals (system-reminder staleness annotations like 'This memory is 2 days old') appear on user-home memory files but are captured by no schema field in the current Wave 1 JSONL spec.
evidence: D4b-2 found recency_signal on 2 files and added the field to its own JSONL output but noted it was not in the spec.

claim: scripts/config/propagation-patterns.seed.json mirrors CLAUDE.md §5 exactly but its consumer detector script was not found. Either the detector was never built or lives in a path Wave 1 did not reach.
evidence: D11 classified propagation-patterns.seed.json as a suspicious near-orphan.

claim: The scan missed scripts/skills/ directory (referenced by skill-audit and skill-creator for per-skill self-audit scripts) and docs/ directory (referenced by skill-creator). Both are within scope gaps for D5 and D9.
evidence: D11 and D1b both flagged these missing directories.

claim: Session-end hardcodes the plan target .planning/jason-os-mvp/PLAN.md. This is fragile if a second planning directory appears, as D1b noted.
evidence: D1b read session-end SKILL.md and found the hardcoded path.

claim: The research-plan-team team file's example invocation uses 'cross-project sync mechanism' as its concrete example, confirming the current project is the canonical use case for the team pattern.
evidence: D2 read the team file body.

claim: commit-tracker.js uses an async subprocess spawn pattern (mid-session-alerts.js) — the only async spawn in the codebase. The target file (scripts/health/lib/mid-session-alerts.js) is absent but the failure is silently swallowed.
evidence: D3 identified the async_spawn. D11 classified mid-session-alerts.js as a MEDIUM severity gap.

claim: SoNash likely has 40-60 composite workflows vs JASON-OS's 15. The GSD system alone is probably 5-10 composites; TDMS adds more. SoNash composite scan needs 3 agents.
evidence: D12 Section 5 Learnings extrapolates from SoNash skill count vs JASON-OS.

claim: The skill portability sanitization reasons are consistent across 9 of 13 skills: cross-references to SoNash-specific skill names in routing tables (documentation-only), absent EXTRACTIONS.md, env vars, and pr-review SONAR_PROJECT_KEY.
evidence: D1a and D1b documented the sanitization themes for each sanitize-then-portable skill.

claim: Memory sync and canonical-memory promotion has no automated trigger. It is the only composite in D12's 15 that operates without a hook event or user slash command.
evidence: D12 composite #15 explicitly states the loading mechanism is built into Claude Code natively.

claim: The Wave 1 unit count is approximately 190: 13 skills + 9 agents/teams + 13 hooks/lib-helpers + 74 memories + 13 scripts + 13 tool files + 4 research sessions + 13 planning artifacts + 20 CI/docs/configs.
evidence: Summed from all 13 Wave 1 agent inventories.

claim: No Stop, SubagentStop, or Notification hooks are wired in JASON-OS. These events are available but unused. SoNash may have them.
evidence: D3 reviewed the full settings.json hooks section.

claim: The jason-os-mvp research session has 26 agents, 67 claims, and 63 sources at HIGH confidence 63, MEDIUM 4, LOW 0. It resolved 5 critical errors via Phase 3.97 gap pursuit.
evidence: D7 read metadata.json for the jason-os-mvp session.

claim: Planning artifacts are consistently project-scoped and not-portable, with one exception: learnings-docs (PR_REVIEW_LEARNINGS.md) contain transferable methodology content and are sanitize-then-portable.
evidence: D8 read all planning artifacts and applied the portability classification.

claim: Semgrep CI workflow is pinned by tag (:1.95.0), not by digest — a known supply-chain hygiene gap. All other GitHub Actions are SHA-pinned.
evidence: D9 read semgrep.yml and noted the tag vs digest distinction.

claim: Three generations of frontmatter patterns exist across the memory corpus: minimal (name+description+type), extended (+status: active), and fully-attributed (+originSessionId). Schema drift requires normalization before canonical promotion.
evidence: D4a and D4b-3 both independently observed the three generations.

claim: D13 surfaced 4 most likely first-pass failure modes if MVP schema proves insufficient: (1) sync tool doesn't know secrets to provision, (2) synced skill whose agent isn't in target, (3) synced tool but forgot to rebuild, (4) state files traveled with hook.
evidence: D13 Section 6 schema-complexity vs usability tension analysis.

claim: The skills chain brainstorm → deep-research → deep-plan → convergence-loop is the canonical workflow order per CLAUDE.md §7 agent/skill triggers. This chain is also the dependency backbone of the research pipeline.
evidence: D11 Cluster A and G. D12 documents all four as separate composites.

claim: feedback_agent_output_files_empty.md is the only machine-scoped memory in the entire corpus — Windows-specific symlink bug and home vs work locale distinction. The mitigation pattern is portable but diagnostic context is not.
evidence: D4b-1 read the file and classified it as machine-scoped.
